data.description:
# BUG: Concurrent access to the ticket store produces corruption (inconsistent compaction snapshots + orphan events)

## Summary

Concurrent writers to the shared `tickets` branch are producing a **corrupted /
internally-inconsistent store**, which should not be possible. The store's design —
per-store `flock` serialization plus an append-only JSONL event log with
last-writer-append semantics and merge-on-push reconciliation — is supposed to make
concurrency safe: writes serialize under the lock, events only append, and divergent
clones converge on replay. Observed behavior contradicts that guarantee.

## Reproduction Steps

Observed live on 2026-07-05 while closing epic REB-601 (`9322-f84a-e047-4471`) with a
second agent session concurrently active on the same store (its commits show
`CREATE/CLAIM/FILE_IMPACT/REVIEW_RESULT/SIGNATURE/COMPACT` on ticket
`4563-d974-5166-419b`, plus `chore: sync events from rebar reconciler` and
`Merge origin/tickets (auto-reconcile during push retry)` commits).

1. Two sessions write to the same store (`tickets` branch), auto-committing + auto-pushing.
2. Session A closes `seedy-edema-groan` (`b043-9490-32ca-4548`) through the completion
   verifier: the event log records `STATUS(closed)` then `SIGNATURE(completion-verifier)`.
   A `COMPACT b043-9490-32ca-4548` event lands afterward (compaction rewrites history into a
   snapshot).
3. Read the ticket back (`rebar show b043-9490-32ca-4548` / `rebar list --parent 9322…`).
4. Run `rebar fsck`.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** Reads reflect the last committed events. `b043` replays to
  `status=closed` (its only status/signature events are the close + completion-verifier
  sign; there is **no** `REOPEN`/`STATUS(open)` event after them). `fsck` reports a clean
  (or near-clean) store. Concurrent writers converge without corruption.
- **Actual:**
  - `rebar show b043` and `rebar list` report `status: open` **while the same ticket
    still carries its `completion-verifier` signature** (a signature only produced at
    close) — an impossible pair. Its event log contains **no reopen event** after the
    close, so the `open` state is a bad **compaction snapshot**, not a real transition.
  - `rebar fsck` reports **2920 issues**, including many `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`
    (snapshot references a source UUID that still exists as a separate EDIT event) and
    `ORPHAN_EVENT` (pre-snapshot event not captured in `source_event_uuids`).
  - Reads flip-flop between invocations (same ticket read as `closed` then `open`),
    indicating nondeterministic replay against an inconsistent snapshot set.
  - Net: a `closed` epic (REB-601) with a child that reads `open` — a graph-integrity
    violation the completion gate is supposed to prevent.

## Suspected Area

The corruption clusters around **compaction/snapshot generation** and the
**merge-during-push reconciliation** path, not the base append path:

- Compaction may snapshot a stale/partial event set when events arrive (or are merged from
  `origin/tickets`) concurrently with the compaction — `source_event_uuids` then omits
  events that still exist independently (`SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` / `ORPHAN_EVENT`), and the
  snapshot's materialized status can predate later events (the `open`-vs-signed
  contradiction).
- The `Merge origin/tickets (auto-reconcile during push retry)` path may interleave a
  remote compaction with local un-compacted events (or vice versa) such that replay is no
  longer deterministic across clones.
- Investigate whether `flock` actually covers the compaction + push/merge critical section
  (vs only individual event appends), and whether it holds across the read-modify-write of a
  snapshot. Note: the running binary also emits `unknown key 'verify.require_ticket_for_commit'
  ignored` — confirm the compaction code path under test is the current build, not a stale one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Root cause identified and documented: the precise interleaving (compaction vs
      concurrent append / origin merge) that yields `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` /
      `ORPHAN_EVENT` and a snapshot status that contradicts later events.
- [ ] A regression test reproduces the corruption deterministically: concurrent
      writers + a compaction (and/or a merge-during-push) drive at least one
      `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`/`ORPHAN_EVENT` or a status/signature contradiction,
      failing before the fix.
- [ ] Fix ensures compaction + push/merge reconciliation is serialized (or otherwise
      made atomic) with event appends so a snapshot's `source_event_uuids` always
      captures every superseded event and replay is deterministic across clones.
- [ ] `flock` (or the equivalent guard) is verified to cover the full
      compaction/snapshot read-modify-write and the merge-during-push critical section.
- [ ] After the fix, replaying the store yields a single consistent status/signature per
      ticket (no `open`-with-completion-signature); `b043-9490-32ca-4548` and REB-601's
      children read consistently across repeated `show`/`list` calls.
- [ ] A remediation/repair path exists for the already-corrupted store: `fsck` on the
      current store no longer reports `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`/`ORPHAN_EVENT` (either a
      `fsck --repair`-style rebuild of snapshots from the event log, or documented manual
      steps), driving the current 2920-issue count to zero for these classes.
data.title: Concurrent access to ticket store produces corruption (inconsistent compaction snapshots + orphan events)
